perturbation.original_text: The first Development Fees payment shall be due forty-five days after the end of the Calendar Quarter in which the first Sale of a Development Product took place. Thereafter, WPD shall furnish to CNS Development Fees no later than forty-five days after the end of each Calendar Quarter for the Sale of Development Products through the end of such Calendar Quarter and shall further furnish CNS with a written statement setting forth an accounting showing the calculation of the Development Fees.
perturbation.changed_text: The first Development Fees payment shall be due thirty days after the end of the Calendar Quarter in which the first Sale of a Development Product took place. Thereafter, WPD shall furnish to CNS Development Fees no later than forty-five days after the end of each Calendar Quarter for the Sale of Development Products through the end of such Calendar Quarter and shall further furnish CNS with a written statement setting forth an accounting showing the calculation of the Development Fees.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the initial payment deadline for Development Fees from forty-five days to thirty days after the end of the Calendar Quarter. The phrase "Thereafter, WPD shall furnish to CNS Development Fees no later than forty-five days after the end of each Calendar Quarter" still exists, contradicting the first payment deadline being thirty days, creating ambiguity on when the first Development Fee is actually due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The first Development Fees payment shall be due thirty days after the end of the Calendar Quarter in which the first Sale of a Development Product took place. Thereafter, WPD shall furnish to CNS Development Fees no later than forty-five days after the end of each Calendar Quarter for the Sale of Development Products through the end of such Calendar Quarter and shall further furnish CNS with a written statement setting forth an accounting showing the calculation of the Development Fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the due date for the first Development Fees payment from 'forty-five days' to 'thirty days' after the end of the Calendar Quarter. However, the subsequent clause stating 'Thereafter, WPD shall furnish to CNS Development Fees no later than forty-five days after the end of each Calendar Quarter' remains unchanged. This creates a direct contradiction because the first payment is due in 'thirty days' while all subsequent payments including the first are due in 'forty-five days', causing an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to any rights herein which survive termination, this Agreement will earlier terminate in its entirety: (i) upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice from either party if the other party materially breaches this Agreement, unless before the end of such thirty (30) calendar day notice period, the breaching party has cured the material default or breach to the non-breaching party's reasonable satisfaction; or (ii) at any time by mutual written agreement between the Parties, subject to any terms herein which survive termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to any rights herein which survive termination, this Agreement will earlier terminate in its entirety: (i) upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice from either party if the other party materially breaches this Agreement, unless before the end of such sixty (60) calendar day notice period, the breaching party has cured the material default or breach to the non-breaching party's reasonable satisfaction; or (ii) at any time by mutual written agreement between the Parties, subject to any terms herein which survive termination.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the cure period for a material breach from thirty days to sixty days, while leaving the initial notice period at thirty days. This creates ambiguity about the timeline for addressing a breach. The conflicting timeframes create uncertainty and disputes regarding termination rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to any rights herein which survive termination, this Agreement will earlier terminate in its entirety: (i) upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice from either party if the other party materially breaches this Agreement, unless before the end of such sixty (60) calendar day notice period, the breaching party has cured the material default or breach to the non-breaching party's reasonable satisfaction; or (ii) at any time by mutual written agreement between the Parties, subject to any terms herein which survive termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the cure period for a material breach from thirty days to sixty days. The contradicted text references the changed text, and thus, the contradiction still exists as the contradictory terms are still thirty and sixty. This inconsistency creates ambiguity about the timeline for addressing a breach and therefore creates a strong in-text contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: WPD hereby agrees to hold harmless and indemnify CNS, its officers, affiliates, employees, and agents (the "CNS Indemnitees") from and against any and all third party claims, demands, causes of actions, costs of suit and reasonable and documented attorney's fees (collectively "Claims") caused by, arising out of, or resulting from WPD's, its employees, agents', affiliates', licensees', sublicensees' or subcontractors' (i) negligence or willful misconduct; (ii) breach of any warranty or representations set forth herein; (iii) breach or alleged breach of third party intellectual property rights; and (iv) use or sale of Development Products.
perturbation.changed_text:
WPD hereby agrees to hold harmless and indemnify CNS, its officers, affiliates, employees, and agents (the "CNS Indemnitees") from and against any and all third party claims, demands, causes of actions, costs of suit, punitive damages and reasonable and documented attorney's fees (collectively "Claims") caused by, arising out of, or resulting from WPD's, its employees, agents', affiliates', licensees', sublicensees' or subcontractors' (i) negligence or willful misconduct; (ii) breach of any warranty or representations set forth herein; (iii) breach or alleged breach of third party intellectual property rights; and (iv) use or sale of Development Products.
WPD's liability for the types of harms detailed above will be capped at the total sum of payments made to WPD under section 2.1.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by expanding the scope of indemnifiable damages to include punitive damages while capping WPD's liability in the new paragraph added. The previous paragraph includes 'Claims' meaning third party claims which are indemnified, however the new paragraph limits WPD's liability to the payments made to it, which contradicts the intention of the first paragraph to have WPD fully indemnify CNS. The lack of clarity regarding whether the limit applies only to direct claims, or to the third-party claims referenced in the original paragraph creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding the extent of WPD's indemnification obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
WPD hereby agrees to hold harmless and indemnify CNS, its officers, affiliates, employees, and agents (the "CNS Indemnitees") from and against any and all third party claims, demands, causes of actions, costs of suit, punitive damages and reasonable and documented attorney's fees (collectively "Claims") caused by, arising out of, or resulting from WPD's, its employees, agents', affiliates', licensees', sublicensees' or subcontractors' (i) negligence or willful misconduct; (ii) breach of any warranty or representations set forth herein; (iii) breach or alleged breach of third party intellectual property rights; and (iv) use or sale of Development Products.
WPD's liability for the types of harms detailed above will be capped at the total sum of payments made to WPD under section 2.1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text commits WPD to full indemnification of CNS for third-party claims, including attorney's fees and other costs. The modified text caps WPD's liability at the payments made to WPD under section 2.1, contradicting the original agreement. The new limitation directly restricts the scope of indemnification, rendering the initial agreement uncertain and potentially unenforceable, as it is unclear if third party claims are included in the limited liability. This adheres to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
